generator: Scite literature enrichment via GPT-5.6 Thinking

operations.value: Rhodiola may reduce some symptoms of stress-related or mental fatigue in selected populations, but the clinical literature is heterogeneous and frequently at risk of bias; the effect should be framed as preliminary rather than established.
operations.notes: Systematic reviews identified signals for fatigue outcomes, but no included study in the 2012 review was free of plausible bias, and a later rigorously conducted nursing-student trial was inconclusive.

operations.value: Rhodiola exercise-performance studies report mixed results across acute and short-term protocols; differences in participants, doses, outcomes, and extract composition prevent a reliable broad ergogenic claim.
operations.notes: Recent systematic reviews describe variable findings and substantial protocol and product heterogeneity; this claim intentionally avoids promising a general endurance or recovery benefit.

operations.value: Clinical studies predominantly use standardized root or root-and-rhizome extracts. Many report approximately 3% rosavins and 1% salidroside, but composition and independent verification vary, so findings should not be generalized to every powder or extract.
operations.notes: Adds product-form specificity without recommending a dose or brand. The sports-review literature repeatedly identifies extract standardization as an important interpretive limitation.

operations.value: Historical Rhodiola trials include unclear randomization, unvalidated outcome measures, inappropriate statistical comparisons, reporting errors, and exaggerated conclusions; evidence summaries should privilege better-controlled trials and systematic reviews.
operations.notes: Evidence-quality limitation drawn from a dedicated critical appraisal and the 2012 systematic review.

sources.title: Rhodiola rosea for physical and mental fatigue: a systematic review
Rhodiola Rosea for Mental and Physical Fatigue in Nursing Students: A Randomized Controlled Trial
Rhodiola rosea supplementation on sports performance: A systematic review of randomized controlled trials
Effects of Rhodiola Rosea Supplementation on Exercise and Sport: A Systematic Review

notes: Second controlled Scite enrichment pass. Adds cautious fatigue, exercise, product-standardization, and evidence-quality context without changing publishing controls, governance status, or evidence grades.
